summary: Mitochondria do not directly contribute to leaf remodeling in lace plants during programmed cell death due to fundamental differences in cellular mechanisms; plants use hormone-driven processes like senescence instead of animal-like caspase-mediated pathways involving mitochondrial outer membrane permeabilization. This distinction highlights that while both organisms exhibit "programmed" cell death, their underlying biological pathways diverge significantly across species.
